content.text: The system shall provide an Undo action control to allow users to revert the most recent supported modification.
I can revert my most recent changes.
the toolbar or action controls are displayed
the system provides an Undo action control.

2.title: Enable Undo When Reversible Actions Exist

content.text: The system shall enable the Undo control only when at least one reversible action exists in the current session.
the Undo control enabled when actions can be reverted
at least one reversible action exists in the current session
the system enables the Undo control

3.title: Disable Undo When No Actions Exist

content.text: The system shall disable the Undo control when no reversible actions are available.
the Undo control disabled when no actions can be undone
the interface accurately reflects available actions.
the system disables the Undo control.
The system shall display a tooltip message "Nothing to undo" when the user hovers over or focuses on a disabled Undo control.
I understand why the action cannot be performed.
the user hovers over or focuses on the control
the system displays the tooltip message “Nothing to undo”.
The system shall revert the most recent eligible action when the user activates the Undo control.
the most recent eligible action reverted
at least one reversible action exists
the user activates the Undo control
the system reverts the most recent eligible action.
The system shall update the interface immediately after a successful Undo operation.
the interface updated immediately after an Undo operation
I can see the reverted state.
the system updates the interface immediately to reflect the reverted state.
The system shall maintain the sequence of reversible actions to support multiple consecutive Undo operations.
I can step back through previous changes.
the user repeatedly activates the Undo control
the system maintains the action history sequence
the system supports multiple (upto 50) consecutive Undo operations.
The system shall visually distinguish enabled and disabled states of the Undo control.
enabled and disabled Undo states to be visually distinct
I can quickly identify whether Undo is available.
the system visually distinguishes enabled and disabled states.
The system shall provide a Redo action control to allow users to restore the most recently undone action.
I can restore previously undone actions.
the system provides a Redo action control.

10.title: Enable Redo When Undone Actions Exist

content.text: The system shall enable the Redo control only when at least one previously undone action is available for restoration.
the Redo control enabled when an action can be restored
I can reapply an undone change.
at least one previously undone action is available
the system enables the Redo control.

11.title: Disable Redo When No Actions Are Available

content.text: The system shall disable the Redo control when no actions are available to be restored.
the Redo control disabled when no actions can be restored
I understand that Redo is unavailable.
the system disables the Redo control.
The system shall display a tooltip message "Nothing to redo" when the user hovers over or focuses on a disabled Redo control.
I understand why the action cannot be performed.
the user hovers over or focuses on the control
the system displays the tooltip message “Nothing to redo”.
The system shall restore the most recently undone action when the user activates the Redo control.
restore the most recently undone action
I can reapply a change if needed.
at least one undone action exists
the user activates the Redo control
the system restores the most recently undone action.
The system shall update the interface immediately after a successful Redo operation.
the interface updated immediately after a Redo operation
I can see the restored state.
the system updates the interface immediately to reflect the restored state.
The system shall maintain the sequence of undone actions to support multiple consecutive Redo operations.
I can restore multiple previously undone changes.
the user repeatedly activates the Redo control
the system maintains the sequence of undone actions and supports multiple consecutive Redo operations.
The system shall visually distinguish enabled and disabled states of the Redo control.
enabled and disabled Redo states to be visually distinct
I can quickly identify whether Redo is available.
the system visually distinguishes enabled and disabled states.

17.title: Clear Redo History After New Action

content.text: The system shall clear the available Redo history when a new user action is performed after an Undo operation.
Redo history cleared when I perform a new action after Undo
one or more actions have been undone
the user performs a new action
the system clears the available Redo history.

18.title: Update Undo and Redo Availability Dynamically

content.text: The system shall dynamically update the availability state of both Undo and Redo controls based on the current action history.
Undo and Redo controls updated based on action history
I always see the correct available actions.
an action, Undo, or Redo operation occurs
the system dynamically updates the availability state of both Undo and Redo controls.
The system shall display contextual tooltip messages corresponding to the current state of the Undo and Redo controls.
contextual tooltip messages for Undo and Redo controls
I understand their current state and availability.
the Undo or Redo control is displayed
the user hovers over or focuses on the control
the system displays a tooltip message appropriate to the current control state. (redo)

20.title: Prevent Execution of Disabled Undo/Redo Actions

content.text: The system shall prevent users from executing Undo or Redo actions when the respective controls are disabled.
disabled Undo and Redo controls to be non-functional
unavailable actions cannot be executed accidentally.
the Undo or Redo control is disabled
the user attempts to activate the control
the system prevents execution of the corresponding Undo or Redo action.

21.title: Maintain Undo/Redo History for Current Session Only

content.text: The system shall maintain Undo and Redo history only for the current active session and shall clear all available Undo/Redo actions when the user refreshes, reloads, or reopens the page, preventing previously performed actions from being undone or redone after the session reset.
Undo and Redo history limited to the current session
previously performed actions cannot be restored after a page refresh or reload.
Undo or Redo actions exist in the current session
the user refreshes, reloads, or reopens the page
the system clears all Undo and Redo history and prevents previously performed actions from being undone or redone.
The system shall display a "Copy Assigned Properties" checkbox within the Translational Repeat dialog to allow users to control whether assigned properties are replicated to generated elements.
a "Copy Assigned Properties" checkbox in the Translational Repeat dialog
I can control whether properties are copied to generated elements.
the Translational Repeat dialog is opened
the system displays a "Copy Assigned Properties" checkbox.

23.title: Enable Copy Assigned Properties by Default

content.text: The system shall enable the "Copy Assigned Properties" checkbox by default when the Translational Repeat dialog is opened.
the Copy Assigned Properties option enabled by default
property assignments are copied unless I choose otherwise.
the Translational Repeat dialog is opened
the system selects the "Copy Assigned Properties" checkbox by default.

24.title: Copy Assigned Properties to Repeated Elements

content.text: When the "Copy Assigned Properties" option is selected, the system shall copy all assigned section mappings, element assignments, and beta-angle assignments from the source element to every newly created repeated element.
assigned properties copied to generated elements
repeated elements inherit the same configuration as the source element.
the "Copy Assigned Properties" option is enabled
the user applies a Translational Repeat operation
the system copies all section mappings, element assignments, and beta-angle assignments from the source element to all generated elements.

When the "Copy Assigned Properties" option is not selected, the system shall create repeated elements without inheriting any section assignments, element assignments, or beta-angle assignments from the source element.
repeated elements created without inherited assignments when property copying is disabled
I can generate clean elements without existing configurations.
the "Copy Assigned Properties" option is disabled
the user applies a Translational Repeat operation
the system creates generated elements without section assignments, element assignments, or beta-angle assignments.

26.title: Apply Property Copy to Current Repeat Operation Only

content.text: The system shall apply the property-copy operation only to the elements generated through the current Translational Repeat action.
property copying applied only to the current repeat action
a Translational Repeat operation is executed
the system applies copied properties only to elements generated by the current repeat action.
The system shall ensure that copied assignments retain the same values and configuration settings as defined on the original source element.
copied assignments to retain their original configuration values
generated elements match the source element configuration.
assignments are copied to generated elements
the system retains the same assignment values and configuration settings as the source element.
The system shall execute the property-copy process automatically when the user clicks "Apply Repeat" and the "Copy Assigned Properties" option is enabled.
property copying performed automatically during repeat generation
I do not need to manually assign properties afterward.
the "Copy Assigned Properties" option is enabled
the system automatically executes the property-copy process.
The system shall display the copied assignments on generated elements immediately after the repeat operation is completed.
copied assignments visible immediately after generation
I can verify the repeated elements were configured correctly.
a repeat operation with property copying has completed
the system shows the copied assignments on the generated elements immediately.
The system shall provide a "Move Selected Nodes" tool that enables users to reposition one or more selected nodes along a single axis by a specified distance.
a Move Selected Nodes tool available
I can reposition selected nodes along a specific axis.
the rack builder interface is displayed
the system provides a Move Selected Nodes tool that allows repositioning one or more selected nodes along a single axis by a specified distance.
The system shall display a tooltip labelled "Move Selected Nodes" when the user hovers over the Move Selected Nodes toolbar icon.
a tooltip displayed for the Move Selected Nodes tool
I can understand the tool's purpose.
the Move Selected Nodes toolbar icon is displayed
the user hovers over the icon
the system displays the tooltip “Move Selected Nodes”.
The system shall open the Move Selected Nodes dialog when the user clicks the Move Selected Nodes toolbar icon.
a Move Selected Nodes dialog to open
I can configure the move operation.
the Move Selected Nodes toolbar icon is displayed
the system opens the Move Selected Nodes dialog.
The system shall display description “Shift the selected nodes along a single axis by the specified distance”. Below the Move selected nodes title.
a description displayed in the Move Selected Nodes dialog
I understand what the operation does.
the Move Selected Nodes dialog is opened
the system displays the description “Shift the selected nodes along a single axis by the specified distance” below the dialog title.
The system shall display a Direction selection control with the options X, Y, and Z within the Move Selected Nodes dialog.
I can choose the axis for node movement.
the Move Selected Nodes dialog is opened
the system displays a Direction selection control with options X, Y, and Z.
The system shall allow the user to select only one direction axis (X, Y, or Z) at a time for the move operation.
select only one movement direction at a time
the move operation is applied along a single axis.
the Direction selection control is displayed
the system allows selection of only one axis (X, Y, or Z) at a time.
The system shall provide a Distance input field for entering the movement value to be applied to the selected nodes.
I can define the movement value.
the Move Selected Nodes dialog is opened
the system provides a Distance input field.
The system shall accept numeric distance values in the Distance field.
I can specify the movement amount accurately.
the Distance input field is displayed
the system accepts numeric distance values only.

38.title: Validate Direction and Distance Before Move

content.text: The system shall require the user to select a Direction and enter a valid Distance before executing the move operation.
validation before executing the move operation
incomplete or invalid configurations cannot be applied.
the Move Selected Nodes dialog is opened
the user attempts to execute the move operation
the system requires a Direction to be selected and valid Distance value to be entered
the system prevents execution until both requirements are satisfied.
The system shall move all currently selected nodes by the specified distance along the selected axis when the user clicks Apply Move.
selected nodes moved by the specified distance
one or more nodes are selected and a valid Direction and Distance are provided
the system moves all selected nodes by the specified distance along the selected axis.
The system shall update the node positions in the model immediately after the move operation is successfully completed.
node positions updated immediately after a move operation
I can see the results of the movement instantly.
a move operation is successfully completed
the system updates the node positions in the model immediately.
The system shall close the dialog and retain the updated node positions after the move operation is successfully applied.
the dialog to close after a successful move operation
I can continue working with the updated model.
a move operation is successfully completed
the system retains the updated node positions.
The system shall close the dialog without modifying any node positions when the user clicks the Cancel button.
cancel the move operation without making changes
the Move Selected Nodes dialog is open
the system closes the dialog and does not modify any node positions.
The system shall close the dialog without applying any changes when the user clicks the Close (X) icon.
close the dialog using the Close (X) icon
I can exit without applying changes.
the Move Selected Nodes dialog is open
the user clicks the Close (X) icon
the system closes the dialog without applying any changes.
The system shall validate the Distance input and prevent execution of the move operation when the entered value is invalid, empty, or non-numeric.
invalid distance values prevented from executing the move operation
only valid movement values are applied.
the Move Selected Nodes dialog is open
the Distance field is empty, invalid, or non-numeric
the system prevents execution of the move operation
The system shall perform the move operation only on the nodes that are selected at the time the user clicks Apply Move.
the move operation applied only to the nodes I have selected
one or more nodes are selected
the system moves only the nodes that are selected at that moment.
The system shall preserve all existing node properties and connectivity relationships while updating only the node coordinates associated with the selected movement axis.
node properties and connectivity retained during movement
the system preserves all existing node properties and connectivity relationships and updates only the coordinate value of the selected movement axis.
The system shall provide a "Mirror" tool that enables users to create or reposition mirrored geometry across a selected global plane.
I can create or reposition mirrored geometry across a selected global plane.
the system provides a Mirror tool for creating or repositioning mirrored geometry across a selected global plane.
The system shall display a tooltip labelled "Mirror" when the user hovers over the Mirror toolbar icon.
a tooltip displayed for the Mirror tool
I can understand the tool's purpose.
the Mirror toolbar icon is displayed
the user hovers over the icon
The system shall open the Mirror Geometry dialog when the user clicks the Mirror toolbar icon.
the Mirror Geometry dialog to open
the Mirror toolbar icon is displayed
the system opens the Mirror Geometry dialog.

50.title: Display Selected Item Count in Mirror Dialog

content.text: The system shall display the number of currently selected items within the dialog description.
see the number of selected items in the Mirror dialog
I know how many items will be affected by the operation.
one or more items are selected
the Mirror Geometry dialog is opened
the system displays the number of currently selected items within the dialog description.
The system shall provide Direction options including Left ↔ Right (X-axis), Front ↔ Back (Y-axis), and Up ↔ Down (Z-axis).
I can select the desired mirror axis.
the Mirror Geometry dialog is opened
the system provides the following direction options:
The system shall allow the user to select only one mirror direction at a time.
select only one mirror direction at a time
the mirror operation is performed along a single axis.
the system allows only one mirror direction to be selected at a time.
The system shall display the corresponding global axis (X, Y, or Z) associated with the selected mirror direction.
I understand which axis the mirror operation will use.
the system displays the associated global axis (X, Y, or Z).
The system shall provide Position options including Absolute and Offset to define the mirror plane location.
position options available for defining the mirror plane
I can control where the mirror operation occurs.
the Mirror Geometry dialog is opened
the system provides Position options: Absolute and Offset.
The system shall allow the user to select only one position mode at a time.
select only one position mode at a time
the user selects a position mode
the system allows only one position mode (Absolute or Offset) to be selected at a time.

56.title: Define Mirror Plane Using Absolute Coordinates

content.text: When Absolute mode is selected, the system shall allow the user to specify the exact coordinate value of the mirror plane along the selected axis.
specify an exact coordinate value for the mirror plane
I can position the mirror plane precisely.
the user configures the mirror plane
the system allows entry of an exact coordinate value along the selected axis.
When Offset mode is selected, the system shall allow the user to define the mirror plane using an offset distance relative to the selected geometry or reference position.
define the mirror plane using an offset value
I can position the plane relative to existing geometry.
the user configures the mirror plane
the system allows entry of an offset distance relative to the selected geometry or reference position.
The system shall display a numeric input field for entering the mirror plane coordinate or offset value.
a numeric input field for the mirror plane value
I can define the coordinate or offset value.
the Mirror Geometry dialog is opened
the system provides a numeric input field for entering the mirror plane coordinate or offset value.
The system shall validate that the mirror plane value entered is a valid numeric value before allowing the mirror operation to be executed.
mirror plane values validated before execution
invalid mirror operations cannot be performed.
a mirror plane value is entered
the user attempts to execute the mirror operation
the system validates that the value is numeric and valid and prevents execution if validation fails.
The system shall provide Mode options including Copy and Move.
I can choose whether to create or reposition geometry.
the Mirror Geometry dialog is opened
the system provides Mode options: Copy and Move.
The system shall allow the user to select only one mirror mode at a time.
select only one mirror mode at a time
the mirror operation executes in a single, clear mode.
the Mirror Geometry dialog is displayed
the user selects a mirror mode
the system allows only one mode (Copy or Move) to be selected at a time.

62.title: Create Mirrored Geometry in Copy Mode

content.text: When Copy mode is selected, the system shall create a mirrored duplicate of the selected geometry while retaining the original geometry.
a mirrored duplicate created while retaining the original geometry
I can create symmetrical geometry without modifying the source.
the user executes the mirror operation
the system creates a mirrored duplicate of the selected geometry and retains the original geometry in its current position.
When Move mode is selected, the system shall reposition the selected geometry to its mirrored location and remove it from its original position.
selected geometry moved to its mirrored location
I can reposition geometry without creating duplicates.
the user executes the mirror operation
the system moves the selected geometry to its mirrored location
the system removes the geometry from its original position.
The system shall provide a "Copy Assigned Properties" checkbox to control whether assigned properties are transferred to mirrored geometry.
a Copy Assigned Properties checkbox available
I can control whether assignments are copied to mirrored geometry.
the Mirror Geometry dialog is displayed
the system displays a "Copy Assigned Properties" checkbox.

65.title: Enable Copy Assigned Properties by Default

content.text: The system shall enable the "Copy Assigned Properties" checkbox by default when the Mirror Geometry dialog is opened.
the Copy Assigned Properties option selected by default
assignments are copied unless I choose otherwise.
the Mirror Geometry dialog is opened
the system enables the "Copy Assigned Properties" checkbox by default.

When Copy Assigned Properties is selected and Copy mode is used, the system shall copy all assigned section assignments, element assignments, and beta-angle assignments from the source geometry to the mirrored geometry.
the mirrored geometry retains the same configuration as the source.
the system copies all section assignments, element assignments, and beta-angle assignments from the source geometry to the mirrored geometry.
When Copy Assigned Properties is not selected, the system shall create mirrored geometry without inheriting section, element, or beta-angle assignments.
mirrored geometry created without inherited assignments when property copying is disabled
the system creates mirrored geometry without inheriting section assignments, element assignments, or beta-angle assignments.

68.title: Disable Property Copy for Move Mode

content.text: The system shall disable or ignore the Copy Assigned Properties option when Move mode is selected, as no duplicate geometry is created.
the Copy Assigned Properties option disabled or ignored in Move mode
irrelevant settings do not affect the operation.
the Mirror Geometry dialog is displayed
the system disables or ignores the Copy Assigned Properties option
The system shall execute the mirror operation when the user clicks Apply Mirror and all required inputs are valid.
the mirror operation executed when all inputs are valid
mirrored geometry can be created or repositioned.
all required mirror inputs are valid
the system executes the mirror operation.

70.title: Update Model View After Mirror Operation

content.text: The system shall update the model view immediately to display the mirrored geometry after the operation is completed successfully.
the model view updated immediately after mirroring
I can verify the results of the operation.
the system updates the model view immediately and displays the mirrored geometry in its resulting position
The system shall ensure that the mirrored geometry is positioned symmetrically relative to the specified mirror plane.
mirrored geometry positioned symmetrically about the selected mirror plane
the mirrored result is geometrically accurate.
a valid mirror plane is defined
the system positions the mirrored geometry symmetrically relative to the specified mirror plane.
The system shall apply the mirror operation only to the geometry selected at the time the user clicks Apply Mirror.
only the currently selected geometry mirrored
one or more geometry entities are selected
the system applies the mirror operation only to the geometry selected at that time.
The system shall prevent execution of the mirror operation when required inputs are missing or invalid.
validation of mirror inputs before execution
invalid mirror operations cannot be performed.
required mirror inputs are missing or invalid
the user attempts to execute the mirror operation
the system prevents execution of the mirror operation
the system displays appropriate validation feedback.

74.title: Enable Apply Mirror Only When Inputs Are Valid

content.text: The system shall keep the Apply Mirror button disabled until all mandatory inputs required for the mirror operation are provided.
the Apply Mirror button enabled only when required inputs are completed
I can execute only valid mirror operations.
the Mirror Geometry dialog is displayed
mandatory inputs are incomplete or invalid
the system keeps the Apply Mirror button disabled.
the system enables the Apply Mirror button.
The system shall close the dialog without applying any changes when the user clicks the Cancel button.
close the Mirror dialog without applying changes
the Mirror Geometry dialog is open
the system does not apply any changes.

76.title: Close Mirror Dialog Using Close Icon

content.text: The system shall close the dialog without applying any changes when the user clicks the Close (X) icon.
close the dialog using the Close (X) icon
I can exit without performing the mirror operation.
the Mirror Geometry dialog is open
the user clicks the Close (X) icon
the system does not apply any changes.

The system shall preserve all geometry attributes, connectivity relationships, and configuration settings during the mirror operation except where explicitly modified by the selected mode and property-copy settings.
geometry properties and relationships preserved during mirroring
the system preserves geometry attributes, connectivity relationships, and configuration settings
Except for changes explicitly defined by the selected mirror mode and property-copy settings.

78.title: Display Delete Option for Selected Entities

content.text: The system shall display a Delete option in the context menu when one or more model entities are selected.
a Delete option available when entities are selected
I can remove unwanted model entities.
one or more model entities are selected
the system displays a Delete option.
When the user clicks Delete, the system shall remove the selected entities from the model.
selected entities removed from the model
I can clean up or modify the model.
one or more model entities are selected
the system removes the selected entities from the model.
The system shall immediately update the model view to reflect the deletion of the selected entities.
the model view updated immediately after deletion
I can see the current state of the model.
the system updates the model view immediately
the deleted entities are no longer displayed.
The system shall apply the delete operation only to the entities selected at the time the user invokes the action.
the delete operation applied only to currently selected entities
one or more model entities are selected
the user invokes the Delete action
the system applies the delete operation only to the entities selected at that time.

82.title: Display Copy Option for Selected Entities

content.text: The system shall display a Copy option in the context menu when one or more model entities are selected.
a Copy option available when entities are selected
one or more model entities are selected
the system displays a Copy option.
When the user clicks Copy, the system shall copy the selected entities and store them in a temporary clipboard for the current session.
selected entities copied to a clipboard
I can paste them later within the session.
one or more model entities are selected
the system copies the selected entities
the system stores them in a temporary clipboard for the current session.
The system shall preserve the geometry, properties, and relationships of copied entities within the clipboard data.
copied entities to retain their data in the clipboard
pasted entities accurately replicate the originals.
entities are copied to the clipboard
the system preserves the geometry, properties, and relationships of the copied entities.
The system shall allow copied entities to be pasted until the clipboard content is replaced or the session ends.
copied entities available for pasting throughout the session
I can reuse copied content multiple times.
no new copy operation is performed and the session remains active
the system retains the clipboard data for paste operations
Until the clipboard content is replaced or the session ends.
The system shall display a Paste option in the context menu.
a Paste option available in the context menu
I can create entities from copied data.
the system displays a Paste option.

87.title: Enable Paste Only When Clipboard Data Exists

content.text: The system shall enable the Paste option only when valid clipboard data exists from a previous copy operation.
Paste enabled only when copied data is available
I can perform valid paste operations.
valid clipboard data exists from a previous copy operation
the system enables the Paste option.
the system disables the Paste option.
When the user clicks Paste, the system shall create new entities using the copied clipboard data.
pasted entities created from clipboard data
I can duplicate existing model entities.
the system creates new entities using the copied clipboard data.

89.title: Assign Unique Identifiers to Pasted Entities

content.text: The system shall assign unique identifiers to all newly pasted entities.
newly pasted entities assigned unique identifiers
they are treated as distinct entities.
entities are created through a paste operation
the system assigns unique identifiers to all newly pasted entities.

90.title: Preserve Geometry and Properties During Paste

content.text: The system shall preserve the copied geometry and associated properties when creating pasted entities.
pasted entities to retain copied geometry and properties
duplicates accurately reflect the source entities.
the user performs a paste operation
the system preserves the copied geometry and associated properties when creating the pasted entities.
The system shall display a Parallel Lines submenu containing From Layout, From Selection, and Inverse options.

92.title: Display Axis Options Under Parallel Line Categories

the user expands From Layout or From Selection
the system displays X Axis, Y Axis, and Z Axis options.

93.title: Select X-Axis Parallel Lines from Layout

all X-axis parallel lines selected from the layout
I can quickly work with horizontally aligned geometry.
the Parallel Lines submenu is displayed
the user selects From Layout → X Axis
the system selects all lines parallel to the global X-axis.

94.title: Select Y-Axis Parallel Lines from Layout

content.text: When the user selects From Layout → Y Axis, the system shall select all lines that are parallel to the global Y-axis.
all Y-axis parallel lines selected from the layout
I can quickly work with geometry aligned to the Y-axis.
the Parallel Lines submenu is displayed
the user selects From Layout → Y Axis
the system selects all lines parallel to the global Y-axis.

95.title: Select Z-Axis Parallel Lines from Layout

content.text: When the user selects From Layout → Z Axis, the system shall select all lines that are parallel to the global Z-axis.
all Z-axis parallel lines selected from the layout
I can quickly work with vertically aligned geometry.
the Parallel Lines submenu is displayed
the user selects From Layout → Z Axis
the system selects all lines parallel to the global Z-axis.

96.title: Select X-Axis Parallel Lines from Selection

content.text: When the user selects From Selection → X Axis, the system shall identify and select all lines that are parallel to the X-axis-oriented line(s) currently selected by the user.
all lines parallel to selected X-axis-oriented lines to be selected
I can extend my selection based on existing geometry.
one or more X-axis-oriented lines are selected
the user selects From Selection → X Axis
the system identifies and selects all lines parallel to the selected X-axis-oriented line(s).

97.title: Select Y-Axis Parallel Lines from Selection

content.text: When the user selects From Selection → Y Axis, the system shall identify and select all lines that are parallel to the Y-axis-oriented line(s) currently selected by the user.
all lines parallel to selected Y-axis-oriented lines to be selected
I can extend my selection based on existing geometry.
one or more Y-axis-oriented lines are selected
the user selects From Selection → Y Axis
the system identifies and selects all lines parallel to the selected Y-axis-oriented line(s).

98.title: Select Z-Axis Parallel Lines from Selection

content.text: When the user selects From Selection → Z Axis, the system shall identify and select all lines that are parallel to the Z-axis-oriented line(s) currently selected by the user.
all lines parallel to selected Z-axis-oriented lines to be selected
I can extend my selection based on existing geometry.
one or more Z-axis-oriented lines are selected
the user selects From Selection → Z Axis
the system identifies and selects all lines parallel to the selected Z-axis-oriented line(s).
When the user clicks Inverse, the system shall invert the current line selection by deselecting currently selected lines and selecting all remaining eligible lines.
I can quickly select all remaining eligible lines.
one or more lines are currently selected
the system deselects the currently selected lines and selects all remaining eligible lines.
The system shall visually highlight all lines selected through the Parallel Lines functionality.
I can clearly identify the results of the selection operation.
lines are selected using the Parallel Lines functionality
the system visually highlights all selected lines.
The system shall display a Visibility submenu containing Isolate Selected, Hide Selected, Invert Visibility, Nodes Only, Lines Only, Plates Only, and Show All options.
I can control the visibility of model entities.
the user opens the Visibility option
the system displays a Visibility submenu containing Isolate Selected, Hide Selected, Invert Visibility, Nodes Only, Lines Only, Plates Only, and Show All options.
When the user selects Isolate Selected, the system shall hide all non-selected entities and display only the selected entities in the model view.
I can focus on specific model elements.
one or more entities are selected
the system hides all non-selected entities and displays only the selected entities in the model view.
When the user selects Hide Selected, the system shall hide all currently selected entities from the model view.
I can temporarily remove them from the model display.
one or more entities are selected
the system hides all currently selected entities from the model view.
When the user selects Invert Visibility, the system shall hide all currently visible entities and display all currently hidden entities.
I can quickly switch between visible and hidden entities.
entities exist in both visible and hidden states
the system hides all currently visible entities and displays all currently hidden entities.
When the user selects Nodes Only, the system shall display only node entities and hide all other supported entity types.
I can focus on node-related geometry.
the model contains multiple entity types
the system displays only node entities and hides all other supported entity types.
When the user selects Lines Only, the system shall display only line entities and hide all other supported entity types.
I can focus on line-related geometry.
the model contains multiple entity types
the system displays only line entities and hides all other supported entity types.
When the user selects Plates Only, the system shall display only plate entities and hide all other supported entity types.
I can focus on plate-related geometry.
the model contains multiple entity types
the system displays only plate entities
the system hides all other supported entity types.
When the user selects Show All, the system shall restore visibility for all hidden entities in the current model.
all hidden entities restored to view
I can return to the complete model display.
one or more entities are hidden
the system restores visibility for all hidden entities in the current model.

109.title: Refresh Model View After Visibility Changes

content.text: The system shall immediately refresh the model view after any visibility action is applied.
the model view updated immediately after visibility actions
I can instantly see the results.
the system immediately refreshes the model view.

110.title: Preserve Entity Data During Visibility Operations

content.text: The system shall preserve entity data and properties when applying visibility operations, modifying only the display state of entities.
visibility actions to affect only display state
the system preserves all entity data and properties
the system modifies only the display state of the affected entities.
The system shall provide a Grids tab within the Elements & Properties panel to allow users to configure and manage grid definitions.
a Grids tab within the Elements & Properties panel
I can configure and manage grid definitions.
the Elements & Properties panel is displayed
the system displays a Grids tab.
The system shall display the total number of grids configured in the model adjacent to the Grids section title.
see the total number of configured grids
I can quickly understand the grid setup.
grid definitions exist in the model
the system displays the total grid count adjacent to the Grids section title.
The system shall display a Letter Axis control that allows users to define whether alphabetical grid labels are assigned to the X-axis or Y-axis.
I can define which axis uses alphabetical labels.
the system displays a Letter Axis control for assigning alphabetical labels to either the X-axis or Y-axis.
The system shall display the currently configured axis mapping (e.g., X → Y) within the Letter Axis control.
see the current axis mapping configuration
I know which axis uses letters and numbers.
the Letter Axis control is displayed
the system shows the currently configured axis mapping (e.g., X → Y).
The system shall apply alphabetical identifiers (A, B, C, …) to the configured letter axis.
alphabetical identifiers applied to the configured letter axis
grid labels follow the selected convention.
a letter axis has been configured
grid labels are generated or updated
the system applies alphabetical identifiers (A, B, C, …) to the configured letter axis.
The system shall apply numeric identifiers (1, 2, 3, …) to the axis not designated as the letter axis.
numeric identifiers applied to the remaining axis
grid labels follow the selected convention.
a letter axis has been configured
grid labels are generated or updated
the system applies numeric identifiers (1, 2, 3, …) to the non-letter axis.
The system shall provide a Letter Start input field for defining the starting alphabetical grid label.
a Letter Start input field available
I can define the starting alphabetical label.
the system provides a Letter Start input field.
The system shall provide a Number Start input field for defining the starting numeric grid label.
a Number Start input field available
I can define the starting numeric label.
the system provides a Number Start input field.
The system shall display a Generate Grids button when no grid definitions exist for the current model.
a Generate Grids button displayed when no grids exist
I can create initial grid definitions.
no grid definitions exist in the model
the system displays a Generate Grids button.
The system shall display an informational message “Click 'Generate grids' above to snapshot the current geometry into reference grid lines." when no grids have been created
I know how to create grids.
no grid definitions have been created
"Click 'Generate grids' above to snapshot the current geometry into reference grid lines."
When the user clicks Generate Grids, the system shall create grid definitions based on the current model geometry.
grid definitions generated from model geometry
I can establish a grid reference system.
no grids exist or the user wants to create grids
the system creates grid definitions based on the current model geometry.
The system shall automatically generate subsequent grid identifiers sequentially based on the configured Letter Start and Number Start values.
identifiers follow a predictable naming convention.
Letter Start and Number Start values are configured
the system creates subsequent grid identifiers sequentially from the configured starting values.
The system shall validate Letter Start and Number Start values before applying grid label updates.
grid start values validated before use
Letter Start or Number Start values are entered
the user generates or updates grids
the system validates the values before applying grid label updates.
Upon successful grid generation, the system shall populate the X-Axis and Y-Axis grid sections with the generated grid lines and corresponding positions.
generated grids displayed within axis sections
I can review generated grid lines and positions.
the system populates the X-Axis and Y-Axis grid sections with generated grid lines and positions.
The system shall remove the empty-state instructional message after grid definitions have been successfully generated.
the empty-state message removed after grid creation
only relevant grid information is displayed.
grid definitions have been successfully generated
the system removes the empty-state instructional message.
The system shall provide a Refresh from Geometry action that regenerates or updates grid definitions based on the current model geometry.
a Refresh from Geometry action available
I can update grids based on the latest model changes.
the system provides a Refresh from Geometry action.
When the user clicks Refresh from Geometry, the system shall recalculate grid positions and labels using the latest model geometry data.
grid positions and labels recalculated from current geometry
grids remain aligned with the model.
the user clicks Refresh from Geometry
the system recalculates grid positions and labels using the latest model geometry data.
The system shall provide a Download Layout SVG (Top View) action.
a Download Layout SVG (Top View) action available
I can export the grid layout.
the system provides a Download Layout SVG (Top View) action.
When the user clicks Download Layout SVG (Top View), the system shall generate and download an SVG file representing the current grid layout from the top-view perspective.
an SVG file generated from the top-view layout
I can use the layout externally.
the user clicks Download Layout SVG (Top View)
the system generates an SVG representation of the current top-view grid layout
the system downloads the SVG file.
The system shall display separate collapsible sections for X-Axis Grids and Y-Axis Grids.
separate collapsible sections for X and Y grids
I can manage grid information efficiently.
the system displays separate collapsible sections for X-Axis Grids and Y-Axis Grids.

131.title: Display Grid Count per Axis Section

content.text: The system shall display the total number of grid lines contained within each axis section header.
see the number of grid lines within each axis section
I can quickly understand the grid distribution.
the X-Axis or Y-Axis section is displayed
the system displays the total number of grid lines in the section header.
The system shall allow users to expand or collapse each axis section independently.
independently expand or collapse grid axis sections
I can manage the displayed information efficiently.
the X-Axis and Y-Axis grid sections are displayed
the user expands or collapses a section
the system updates only the selected section's visibility state and the other section remains unchanged.

133.title: Display Grid Lines in Expanded Axis Section

content.text: When an axis section is expanded, the system shall display all grid lines associated with that axis.
view all grid lines within an expanded axis section
an axis section contains grid lines
the system displays all grid lines associated with that axis.
The system shall display the grid identifier and corresponding position value for each grid line.
see the identifier and position of each grid line
I can accurately reference grid locations.
a grid line entry is rendered
the system displays the grid identifier and corresponding position value.
The system shall provide an Edit action for each individual grid line.
an Edit action for each grid line
I can modify individual grid definitions.
a grid line entry is rendered
the system provides an Edit action for the grid line.
When the user clicks the Edit icon for a grid line, the system shall allow modification of the selected grid line’s properties.
I can update grid definitions as needed.
the user clicks the Edit action
the system allows modification of the selected grid line’s properties.
The system shall update the displayed grid information immediately after a grid line is modified and saved.
updated grid information displayed immediately after saving changes
a grid line has been modified and saved
the system updates the displayed grid information immediately.
The system shall maintain unique grid identifiers within each axis after grid labels are generated or modified.
grid identifiers to remain unique within each axis
grid labels are generated or modified
the system ensures all grid identifiers remain unique within the corresponding axis.
The system shall preserve all configured grid settings until explicitly modified by the user or regenerated through the Refresh from Geometry function.
configured grid settings retained until explicitly changed
the user continues working in the model
the system preserves all configured grid settings until the user modifies them or executes Refresh from Geometry.
The system shall synchronize grid label updates across the model view and all dependent grid-related functions after changes are applied.
grid label changes reflected throughout the system
the system synchronizes the updated grid labels across the model view and all dependent grid-related functions.
